title: Align defensive service.close() try/except across all maintenance entrypoints
analysis:
The three maintenance entrypoints (run_reindex, run_cleanup, run_verify_zombie_edges) share an identical finally-block pattern: close the MemoryService, then restore CONFIG_PATH. However, only reindex.py (lines 198-201) wraps service.close() in try/except to prevent a close() failure from blocking CONFIG_PATH restoration. cleanup_stale_edges.py (line 134) and verify_zombie_edges.py (line 174) have bare `await service.close()` calls — if close() raises, the CONFIG_PATH restoration code below it is skipped, violating the env-var invariant.

The fix is straightforward: wrap the bare service.close() calls in the same try/except pattern used in reindex.py, and add a comment explaining the purpose across all three files. Existing tests verify that service.close() is called (test_closes_service_on_success, test_closes_service_on_error), but no tests verify that CONFIG_PATH is still restored when close() itself raises. New tests must cover this critical behavior for cleanup_stale_edges.py and verify_zombie_edges.py, and also verify the new log warning message is emitted.

The existing test for test_closes_service_on_success in TestRunCleanup (line 341) asserts `mock_service.close.assert_awaited_once()`. After our change, close() is still called but now wrapped in try/except. The assertion remains valid because AsyncMock.close will be awaited inside the try block. The test_closes_service_on_error tests also remain valid — they test that close() runs even when the main operation raises, which is orthogonal to what happens when close() itself raises.

steps.description: Write failing test in test_cleanup_stale_edges.py: add test_restores_config_path_when_close_raises to TestRunCleanupEnvVarRestore. Save original CONFIG_PATH, create mock_service with close=AsyncMock(side_effect=RuntimeError('close error')), patch FusedMemoryConfig, MemoryService (returns mock_service), and CleanupManager (returns mock with successful cleanup). Call run_cleanup(config_path='test.yaml'). Assert os.environ.get('CONFIG_PATH') == original after the call completes (the close error should not propagate since run_cleanup returns a result from the try block). Wrap in try/finally for test safety. This WILL FAIL because bare `await service.close()` at line 134 raises and skips CONFIG_PATH restoration.

Write failing test in test_cleanup_stale_edges.py: add new class TestRunCleanupCloseWarning with test_logs_warning_when_close_raises. Create mock_service with close=AsyncMock(side_effect=RuntimeError('close error')), patch all constructors to succeed, use caplog at WARNING level for 'fused_memory.maintenance.cleanup_stale_edges' logger, call run_cleanup(). Assert a WARNING record containing 'Error closing service during run_cleanup cleanup' was logged. This WILL FAIL because the bare await has no try/except and no warning logging.

Write failing test in test_verify_zombie_edges.py: add test_restores_config_path_when_close_raises to TestRunVerifyZombieEdgesEnvVarRestore. Save original CONFIG_PATH, create mock_service with close=AsyncMock(side_effect=RuntimeError('close error')), patch FusedMemoryConfig, MemoryService (returns mock_service), and ZombieEdgeVerifier (returns mock with successful cleanup). Call run_verify_zombie_edges(uuids=['test-uuid'], config_path='test.yaml'). Assert CONFIG_PATH is restored. This WILL FAIL because bare `await service.close()` at line 174 raises and skips restoration.

Write failing test in test_verify_zombie_edges.py: add new class TestRunVerifyZombieEdgesCloseWarning with test_logs_warning_when_close_raises. Create mock_service with close=AsyncMock(side_effect=RuntimeError('close error')), patch all constructors, use caplog at WARNING level, call run_verify_zombie_edges(uuids=['test-uuid']). Assert WARNING containing 'Error closing service during run_verify_zombie_edges cleanup' was logged. This WILL FAIL because the bare await has no try/except.

In reindex.py run_reindex() finally block (line 197), add the inline comment `# Catch close() errors so CONFIG_PATH restoration below always runs.` above the existing try block at line 198. The try/except already exists here from the Task 143 fix — this step only adds the documenting comment to complete the three-way alignment.

Run full test suite for all three modules: pytest fused-memory/tests/test_reindex.py fused-memory/tests/test_cleanup_stale_edges.py fused-memory/tests/test_verify_zombie_edges.py. Verify all existing tests pass (no regressions) and all new tests pass. Confirm the three finally blocks are now structurally identical: `if service is not None: # comment \n try: await service.close() \n except Exception: logger.warning(...)` followed by CONFIG_PATH restoration.

design_decisions.decision: Match the exact try/except pattern from reindex.py rather than extracting a shared helper
design_decisions.rationale: The task description explicitly says to apply the same pattern. Extracting a helper (e.g. _safe_close()) would be a scope expansion beyond what's requested and would touch more code than necessary. The inline pattern is only 4 lines and is already established in reindex.py.

design_decisions.decision: Use function-specific warning messages (e.g. 'during run_cleanup cleanup' vs 'during run_verify_zombie_edges cleanup')
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the existing reindex.py pattern which uses 'during run_reindex cleanup'. Function-specific messages make it easier to identify which entrypoint had the close() failure in production logs.

design_decisions.decision: Test CONFIG_PATH restoration when close() raises, not just that close() is called
design_decisions.rationale: The whole point of this try/except is to protect the CONFIG_PATH restoration that follows. Existing tests only verify service.close() is called. Testing the actual invariant (env var restored even when close fails) is the high-value behavioral test that proves the fix works.

design_decisions.decision: Add inline comment to all three files including existing reindex.py
design_decisions.rationale: Per the task description, the comment documents that the try/except is load-bearing for the CONFIG_PATH invariant, not merely defensive. Adding it to reindex.py (which already has the try/except but not the comment) completes the documentation alignment across all three entrypoints.

design_decisions.decision: Test the warning log message in addition to CONFIG_PATH restoration
design_decisions.rationale: The warning log is the only observable indicator that close() failed silently. Without it, close() failures would be invisible in production. Testing its presence ensures the pattern is complete, not just structurally present but functionally logging.

reuse.how: Copy the exact try/except Exception block with logger.warning('Error closing service during run_<name> cleanup', exc_info=True) into the finally blocks of cleanup_stale_edges.py and verify_zombie_edges.py, substituting the function name in the message.

reuse.what: ENV var restoration test pattern from TestRunCleanupEnvVarRestore
reuse.how: New close-error tests follow the same structure: save original CONFIG_PATH, set up mocks with patches, call entrypoint with config_path='test.yaml', assert CONFIG_PATH == original, wrap in try/finally safety net. The key addition is patching mock_service.close with side_effect=RuntimeError.

reuse.what: Mock service setup from TestRunCleanup and TestRunVerifyZombieEdges
reuse.how: Reuse the same AsyncMock() pattern for mock_service, the same patch context managers for FusedMemoryConfig/MemoryService/Manager classes, and the same CleanupResult/VerifyResult mock returns.
